rationale: Ground truth has no substantive concerns (just LGTM/approvals), so both trivially cover it and neither fabricates anything obviously wrong — both verify the PR's core claims against source with specific file/line citations. X's only 'comment' is hedged and ultimately recommends keeping the current wording, offering little actionable value beyond validating the PR. Y goes further with a repo-wide sweep and surfaces a genuine, concretely actionable gap (stale extra_body wording in examples/online_serving/minicpmo/README.md:58, with a quoted line and suggested fix), giving it an edge on actionability and added value despite similar precision.
